full_text:
**Experimental Plan**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) (10x stock, #07912, STEMCELL
 > Technologies, stored in the wet lab freezer Enzymes box) for 1
 > hour

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Dispase (50 U/ml stock, #D4693-1G, Sigma Merck, stored in the wet
 > lab fridge) for 30 min followed by C&H for 1 hour.

- This approach includes dispase-only condition.

Media used in this protocol is DMEM/F12+++, which is DMEM/F12
(#11320033, Gibco) supplemented with 1x GlutaMAX (#35050061, Gibco), 10
nM HEPES (#H0887, Sigma-Aldrich) and 1% penstrep
(Penicillin-Streptomycin, 10,000 U/mL, #15140122, Gibco). Media is
premade in the cell culture room fridge.

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collect ovarian tumor samples and wash larger tumor pieces by
 > placing them into a 50 ml Falcon tube, pouring sterile HBSS
 > (#14175-053, Gibco) on top, and gently inverting the tube several
 > times.

- Mince the tumor piece into small pieces (using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors on a 10 cm cell culture plate.

- Divide the minced tissue into 2 equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Dispase followed by collagenase condition\*:

el and
 > scissors on a 10 cm cell culture plate.

- Divide the minced tissue into 2 equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Dispase followed by collagenase condition\*:

- Treat the first half of the minced sample with dispase for 30
 > minutes at +37°C rotating. Vortex (approximately 15 seconds)
 > before, in the middle, and after incubation.

 - Prepare dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl
 > dispase (50 U/ml stock) + 100 µl RQ1 RNase-Free DNase (#M6101,
 > Promega, stored wet lab freezer Enzymes box), and place the
 > tissue pieces in.

- Filter the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer (#22-363-548, Fisher)
 > to obtain single-cell suspensions.

 - Pre-wet the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the digested
 > suspension through the filter into a 50 ml Falcon tube, wash
 > the stainer with 5 ml media, and **keep the tissue
 > leftovers.**

- **For obtained cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - Do the Red Blood Cell (RBC) lysis (#11814389001, Sigma-Aldrich):
 > add 2-4 ml depending on the pellet size, mix thoroughly,
 > incubate 5 min RT, and add 10-20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++ media.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C, and
 > resuspend to DMEM/F12+++.

 - Count the cells and prepare the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry/biobanking.

a.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C, and
 > resuspend to DMEM/F12+++.

 - Count the cells and prepare the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry/biobanking.

- **Further, dissociate the leftover tissue pieces with C&H** for 1 h
 > at +37°C rotating. Vortex before and after the incubation period.

 - Prepare C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml
 > C&H (10x stock) + 100 µl RQ1 RNase-Free DNase, and place the
 > tissue pieces in.

- Filter the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer to obtain single-cell
 > suspensions

 - Pre-wet the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the digested
 > suspension through the filter into a 50 ml Falcon tube, wash
 > the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the tissue leftovers.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- Do the RBC lysis: add 2-4 ml depending on the pellet size, mix
 > thoroughly, incubate 5 min RT, and add 10-20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media.

- Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C, and
 > resuspend to DMEM/F12+++.

- Count the cells and prepare the aliquots for flow cytometry.

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only condition\*:

- Treat the second half of the tissue pieces with C&H for 1 hour at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortex (approximately 15 seconds) before, in the
 > middle, and after incubation.

ition\*:

- Treat the second half of the tissue pieces with C&H for 1 hour at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortex (approximately 15 seconds) before, in the
 > middle, and after incubation.

 - Prepare C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml
 > C&H (10x stock) + 100 µl RQ1 RNase-Free DNase, and place the
 > tissue pieces in.

- Filter the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer to obtain single-cell
 > suspensions

 - Pre-wet the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the digested
 > suspension through the filter into a 50 ml Falcon tube, wash
 > the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the tissue leftovers.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- Do the RBC lysis: add 2-4 ml depending on the pellet size, mix
 > thoroughly, incubate 5 min RT, and add 10-20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media.

- Centrifuge the cell suspension for 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C, and
 > resuspend to DMEM/F12+++.

- Count the cells and prepare the aliquots for flow cytometry.

3\. \*Flow Cytometry\*:

Prepare aliquots from each dissociation method (3) for multicolor
staining, and from the Dispase + C&H dissociated cells, prepare FMOs (8)
and an unstained control. Each aliquot should contain up to 1.5 × 10⁶
cells.

- Wash cells once by adding 900 µl FACS buffer (2 % FBS, 2 mM EDTA in
 > PBS, stored in the wet lab fridge) and centrifuge 6 min 400 g at
 > +4°C (you may increase to 450 g if a pellet is not forming).

- Surface antibody staining

FBS, 2 mM EDTA in
 > PBS, stored in the wet lab fridge) and centrifuge 6 min 400 g at
 > +4°C (you may increase to 450 g if a pellet is not forming).

- Surface antibody staining

 - For multicolor samples, and a-SMA and PDGFRb FMO samples:
 > Calculate the required amounts of antibodies based on the
 > table above and prepare an antibody mix by diluting them in
 > Brilliant Stain Buffer (#563794, BD Biosciences, stored in the
 > wet lab fridge). Add 50 µl of the antibody mix to each
 > multicolor sample.

 - For FMO samples: First, add the required amount of Brilliant
 > Stain Buffer to each sample, as indicated in the table above.
 > Then, add each antibody one at a time, excluding the antibody
 > corresponding to the fluorophore being tested in the FMO
 > sample.

 - Incubate for 30 min at RT shielded from light.

- After incubation, wash once with 900 µl FACS buffer (centrifuge 6
 > min 400 g at +4°C).

- Fix cells by adding 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222, Invitrogen,
 > stored in the wet lab fridge) and incubate for 40 min at +4°C
 > shielded from light.

- After incubation, add 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer (#00-8333-56,
 > Invitrogen, diluted in mQ-water, stored in the wet lab fridge),
 > and incubate 5 min at +4°C.

- Centrifuge 6 min 400 g at +4°C, wash pellet with 1 ml 1x
 > Permeabilization buffer, and resuspend in 900 µl FACS buffer for
 > overnight storage.

> and incubate 5 min at +4°C.

- Centrifuge 6 min 400 g at +4°C, wash pellet with 1 ml 1x
 > Permeabilization buffer, and resuspend in 900 µl FACS buffer for
 > overnight storage.

- Next day, start by centrifuging 6 min 400 g at +4°C.

- Intracellular antibody staining

 - For multicolor samples and FMOs samples excluding a-SMA and
 > PDGFRb: Calculate the required amounts of antibodies based on
 > the table above and prepare an antibody mix by diluting them
 > in 1x Permeabilization buffer. Add 50 µl of the antibody mix
 > to each sample.

 - For a-SMA and PDGFRb FMO samples: First, add the required amount
 > of 1x Permeabilization buffer to each sample, as indicated in
 > the table above. Then, add the remaining antibody to the
 > cells: add a-SMA antibody to the PDGFRb FMO sample and PDGFRb
 > antibody to the a-SMA FMO sample.

 - Incubate for 30 min at RT shielded from light.

- Wash once with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer (centrifuge 6 min 400
 > g at +4°C) and resuspend in 400 µl FACS buffer.

- Pipeted the cell suspension through a 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube
 > with a cell-strainer cap (blue) (#352235 Corning). Avoid bubbles.

- Keep the samples on ice and protected from light until running at
 > Fortessa.

Prepare compensation controls

- Use Falcon 5 ml polystyrene round-bottom tubes (white cap) (#352052
 > Corning).

s on ice and protected from light until running at
 > Fortessa.

Prepare compensation controls

- Use Falcon 5 ml polystyrene round-bottom tubes (white cap) (#352052
 > Corning).

- Prepare one unstained and for each fluorophore one compensation
 > tube.

- Use BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads (#560497, BD
 > bioscience, stored in the wet lab fridge), first vortex gently and
 > then add one drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
 > #51-9006274) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-9006227)
 > compensation beads to each tube.

- For stained compensation controls, add 2 µl of each antibody, except
 > for a-SMA, for which 2.5 µl should be added. DCM cannot be used
 > for compensation controls, so it should be replaced with an
 > antibody labeled with the same fluorophore (BV510).

- Incubate for at least 30 min at +4°C shielded from light, and
 > resuspend to 350 µl FACS buffer.

Analyze the samples to determine the percentage of fibroblasts (CD45-
EpCAM- CD31- cells).
